2603.22158.title: Multimodal Survival Analysis with Locally Deployable Large Language Models
2603.22158.paper_summary: We study multimodal survival analysis integrating clinical text, tabular covariates, and genomic profiles using locally deployable large language models (LLMs). As many institutions face tight computational and privacy constraints, this setting motivates the use of lightweight, on-premises models. Our approach jointly estimates calibrated survival probabilities and generates concise, evidence-grounded prognosis text via teacher-student distillation and principled multimodal fusion. On a TCGA cohort, it outperforms standard baselines, avoids reliance on cloud services and associated privacy concerns, and reduces the risk of hallucinated or miscalibrated estimates that can be observed in base LLMs.
2603.22158.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究整合臨床文本、表格協變數和基因組特徵的多模態生存分析，使用可在本地部署的大型語言模型（LLMs）。由於許多機構面臨嚴格的計算和隱私限制，這種情境促使我們使用輕量級的本地模型。我們的方法共同估計經校準的生存概率，並通過教師-學生蒸餾和原則性多模態融合生成簡明的、基於證據的預後文本。在TCGA隊列中，它的表現超過了標準基準，避免了對雲服務及相關隱私問題的依賴，並降低了在基礎LLMs中可能觀察到的幻覺或錯誤校準估計的風險。
2603.22158.author: Moritz Gögl et.al.
